claim.hypotheses.explanation: Captures the core prediction that risk-associated frames reduce WTP and that WTA is not affected by framing, but adds additional elements (effects of high/low outcomes) beyond the specific lottery vs. gift card hypothesis.

claim.hypotheses_location.explanation: Matches the reference that hypotheses are discussed in the abstract/introduction and detailed in the theoretical background.

claim.statement.explanation: Provides the core claim (framing influences WTP but not WTA) but lacks the specific comparison (lottery vs. gift certificate) emphasized in the reference.

claim.statement_location.explanation: Points to the title/abstract, whereas the reference specifies Figure 1 and the Experiment 1 results section.

claim.study_type.explanation: Both identify the study as Experimental.

data.source.explanation: Extracted specifies MTurk recruitment; the reference lists source as NA.

data.sample_size.explanation: Extracted lists multiple sample sizes including Experiment 1 as N=119, whereas the reference specifies N=101.

data.unit_of_analysis.explanation: Both denote the individual as the unit of analysis.

data.access_details.explanation: Both note an online supplement link and no data access details.

data.notes.explanation: Includes relevant caveats (exclusions, nonparametric tests), but also asserts random assignment and other details not stated in the reference, which notes no mention of randomization.

method.description.explanation: Conveys the experimental manipulation of frames and assessment of WTP/WTA, consistent with the reference, but spans multiple experiments and adds more details.

method.steps.explanation: Covers recruitment, assignment, instructions, comprehension checks, incentive mechanism, and analyses similar to the reference, but with broader scope and different ordering/details.

method.models.explanation: Reference highlights t-tests and z-tests (Experiment 1), while the extracted adds ANOVA and nonparametric tests used across other experiments.

method.outcome_variable.explanation: Includes WTP (matches reference) and WTA (broader than the specific reference focus).

method.independent_variables.explanation: Reference specifies lottery vs. gift certificate; extracted includes additional variables (payoff outcomes, status, objective uncertainty).

method.control_variables.explanation: Reference states not stated; extracted lists covariates and checks.

method.tools_software.explanation: Extracted notes software not stated (aligns for analysis), but omits the MTurk recruitment tool mentioned in the reference.

results.summary.explanation: Accurately summarizes that risk-associated frames reduce WTP and not WTA, but extends beyond Experiment 1 into later experiments.

results.numerical_results[0].outcome_nam.explanation: Identifies Experiment 1 WTP comparison between lottery and gift certificate, consistent with the reference.

results.numerical_results[0].value.explanation: Means and SDs match the reference (M=$5.68 vs. $9.52; SDs match).

results.numerical_results[0].effect_size.explanation: Partial eta-squared = .11 matches the reference (reported alongside the t-test).

results.numerical_results[0].p_value.explanation: p < .001 matches the t-test p-value reported in the reference.

results.numerical_results[0].statistical.explanation: Correctly marked as significant, aligning with the reference.

results.numerical_results[0].direction.explanation: Extracted describes a negative effect of risk framing on WTP, whereas the reference encodes the t-statistic direction as positive.

results.numerical_results[1].outcome_nam.explanation: Covers Experiment 2 main effects, which are not present in the reference.

results.numerical_results[1].value.explanation: Experiment 2 F-statistics are not in the reference.

results.numerical_results[1].unit.explanation: No corresponding unit/info in the reference.

results.numerical_results[1].effect_size.explanation: Effect sizes for Experiment 2 are not in the reference.

results.numerical_results[1].confidence_.explanation: Not available in the reference for this result.
